blurb: The rules in this category are known in Pali as pācittiya, “entailing confession”. There are one hundred and sixty-six such rules for the nuns, seventy of which are shared with the monks. This is the largest class of rules in the Monastic Code. These rules, together with all the other rules apart from those entailing expulsion and suspension, are classed as light offenses, lahukāpatti. For light offenses there is no penalty apart from confession.

children.translated_name: 1. The training rule on eating garlic
children.blurb: A nun who eats raw garlic must confess the offense. This rule includes the Suvaṇṇahaṁsa Jātaka tale, “the Golden goose Jātaka,” found in slightly different form in the Jātaka collection.
A nun who removes hair from the armpits or the groin must confess the offense.
A nun who slaps her genitals must confess the offense.
A nun who uses a dildo must confess the offense.
A nun who inserts more than two joints of the fingers into the vagina must confess the offense.
A nun who attends on a monk while he is eating must confess the offense.
A nun who asks for raw grain and then prepares and eats it must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 8. Disposing of waste over a wall
children.blurb: A nun who disposes of feces, urine, or rubbish over a wall must confess the offense.
A nun who disposes of feces, urine, or rubbish on cultivated plants must confess the offense.
A nun who goes to see a show must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 11. Meeting a man in the dark
children.blurb: A nun who, seeking privacy, stands alone with a man at night must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 12. Meeting a man in a concealed place
children.blurb: A nun who, seeking privacy, stands alone with a man in a concealed place must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 13. Meeting a man in the open
children.blurb: A nun who, seeking privacy, stands alone with a man out in the open must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 14. Meeting a man on a street
children.blurb: A nun who, seeking privacy, stands alone with a man on street must confess the offense.
A nun who leaves a house without informing the owners must confess the offense.
A nun who sits or lies down on a seat in a house without asking the owners for permission must confess the offense.
A nun who spreads out bedding in a house and then sits or lies down without asking the owners for permission must confess the offense.
A nun who criticizes someone without reflection must confess the offense.
A nun who curses herself or another must confess the offense.
A nun who cries and beats herself must confess the offense.
A nun who bathes naked must confess the offense.
A nun who makes a bathing robe that is too large must confess the offense.
A nun who does not sew back together the robe of another nun after unstitching it must confess the offense.
A nun who leaves any of her five robes untouched for five days must confess the offense.
A nun who wears a borrowed robe without the permission of the owner must confess the offense.
A nun who hinders an order of nuns from receiving robe-cloth must confess the offense.
A nun who blocks an appropriate distribution of robe-cloth must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 28. Giving a robe to a non-monastic
children.blurb: A nun who gives a robe to anyone who is not a Buddhist monastic must confess the offense.
A nun who hinders the distribution of robe-cloth without a proper reason must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 30. Blocking the ending of the robe season
children.blurb: A nun who inappropriately blocks the Order from ending the robe-making season must confess the offense.
A nun who lies down on the same bed as another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who shares bedding with another nun must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 33. Making a nun ill at ease
children.blurb: A nun who intentionally makes another nun ill at ease must confess the offense.
A nun who does not attend on a sick monastic disciple must confess the offense.
A nun who gives a dwelling place to another nun and then throws her out must confess the offense.
A nun who persistently engages in inappropriate socialization with lay people must confess the offense.
A nun who goes wandering without a group of travelers in a dangerous area of her own country must confess the offense.
A nun who goes wandering without a group of travelers in a dangerous area abroad must confess the offense.
A nun who goes wandering during the rainy season must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 40. Not wandering after the rainy season
children.blurb: A nun who does not go wandering after the rainy season must confess the offense.
A nun who goes to see a pleasure house or a pleasure garden must confess the offense.
A nun who uses a large or luxurious seat must confess the offense.
A nun who spins yarn must confess the offense.
A nun who serves a lay person must confess the offense.
A nun who agrees to settle a legal issue but then makes no effort to do so must confess the offense.
A nun who gives food to anyone who is not a Buddhist monastic must confess the offense.
A nun who uses a communal menstruation robe beyond the intended period must confess the offense.
A nun who goes wandering without giving up her lodging must confess the offense.
A nun who studies pointless knowledge must confess the offense.
A nun who teaches pointless knowledge must confess the offense.
A nun who enters a monks’ monastery without permission must confess the offense.
A nun who abuses a monk must confess the offense.
A nun who in anger abuses an order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 54. Eating more after refusing an offer
children.blurb: A nun who eats after finishing her meal must confess the offense.
A nun who tries to keep her supporters from supporting others must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 56. Spending the rainy season apart from monks
children.blurb: A nun who spends the rainy season in a monastery without monks must confess the offense.
A nun who does not invite correction from both Orders after the rainy season must confess the offense.
A nun who does not go to the instruction or formal meetings of the Order must confess the offense.
A nun who does not ask the Order of monks every half-month about the observance day and the going for instruction must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 60. Getting medical treatment from a man
children.blurb: A nun who is alone with a man and gets treatment from him on the thigh must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains a pregnant woman must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains a woman who is breastfeeding must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains a trainee nun who has not completed her training must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 64. Ordaining a trained candidate without approval
children.blurb: A nun who ordains a trainee nun without the approval of the Order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 65. Ordaining a married girl under twelve
children.blurb: A nun who ordains a married girl less than twelve years old must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains a married girl who has not completed her training must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 67. Ordaining a trained married girl without approval
children.blurb: A nun who ordains a married girl without the approval of the Order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 68. Not guiding a disciple after ordination
children.blurb: A nun who ordains a student but does not guide her for two years must confess the offense.
A nun who does not follow her preceptor for two years must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 70. Not sending away a new disciple
children.blurb: A nun who ordains a student without afterwards taking her away must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 71. Ordaining an unmarried woman under twenty
children.blurb: A nun who ordains an unmarried girl less than twenty years old must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains an unmarried girl who has not completed her training must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 73. Ordaining a trained unmarried woman without approval
children.blurb: A nun who ordains an unmarried girl without the approval of the Order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 74. Giving ordination while under twelve years’ seniority
children.blurb: A nun with insufficient seniority who gives ordination must confess the offense.
A nun who gives ordination without the approval of the Order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 76. Agreeing to stop ordaining, but then criticizing it
children.blurb: A nun who agrees when she is refused permission to give ordination but later criticizes it must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 77. Not ordaining a trainee after promising
children.blurb: A nun who promises to ordain a trainee nun in return for robe-cloth but then makes no effort to get her ordained must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 78. Not ordaining a trainee who has followed one
children.blurb: A nun who promises to ordain a trainee nun in return for being attended on for two years but then makes no effort to get her ordained must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 79. Ordaining one who is difficult to live with
children.blurb: A nun who ordains a trainee nun who socializes inappropriately and is difficult to live with must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains a trainee nun who has not been given permission by her parents and husband must confess the offense.
A nun who ordains a trainee nun after the consent of the Order has expired must confess the offense.
A nun who gives ordination in consecutive years must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 83. Ordaining two in the same year
children.blurb: A nun who ordains two women in one year must confess the offense.
A nun who uses a sunshade and sandals must confess the offense.
A nun who travels in a vehicle must confess the offense.
A nun who wears a hip ornament must confess the offense.
A nun who wears ornaments must confess the offense.
A nun who bathes with scents and colors must confess the offense.
A nun who bathes with scents and oil-seed flowers must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 90. Getting a massage from a nun
children.blurb: A nun who makes another nun rub her must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 91. Getting a massage from a trainee nun
children.blurb: A nun who makes a trainee nun rub her must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 92. Getting a massage from a novice nun
children.blurb: A nun who makes a novice nun rub her must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 93. Getting a massage from a female householder
children.blurb: A nun who makes a laywoman rub her must confess the offense.
A nun who sits down in front of a monk without asking permission must confess the offense.
A nun who asks a monk a question without obtaining leave must confess the offense.
A nun who enters an inhabited area without a bust cover must confess the offense.
A nun who lies must confess the offense.
A nun who verbally abuses another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who spreads malicious gossip about another nun must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 100. Instructing a lay person to memorize the Teaching
children.blurb: A nun who teaches an unordained person to learn the word of the Buddha by heart must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 101. Lying down with one not fully ordained
children.blurb: A nun who lies down in the same room as an unordained person for more than three nights must confess the offense.
A nun who lies down in the same room as a man must confess the offense.
A nun who teaches a man more than six sentences of the word of the Buddha must confess the offense.
A nun who tells an unordained person about her own profound spiritual attainment must confess the offense.
A nun who tells an unordained person about another nun’s serious offense must confess the offense.
A nun who digs the earth must confess the offense.
A nun who intentionally destroys vegetation must confess the offense.
A nun who speaks evasively or remains silent when questioned by the Order must confess the offense.
A nun who criticizes or denounces an official of the Order must confess the offense.
A nun who abandons furniture belonging to the Order outdoors must confess the offense.
A nun who abandons bedding in a dwelling belonging to the Order must confess the offense.
A nun who lies down in a dwelling belonging to the Order, encroaching on another nun and aiming to make her depart, must confess the offense.
A nun who throws another nun out of a dwelling belonging to the Order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 114. Sitting down on an upper story
children.blurb: A nun who uses a bed with unfastened legs on an unplanked upper storey of a hut belonging to the Order must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 115. Applying excessive materials to a large dwelling
children.blurb: A nun who applies more than three layers of building material when building a large dwelling must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 116. Pouring water with living beings in it
children.blurb: A nun who pours water containing living beings onto the ground must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 117. Eating excessively at a public guesthouse
children.blurb: A nun who eats more than one meal at a public alms center must confess the offense.
A nun who eats as part of a specifically invited group of nuns must confess the offense.
A nun who accepts more than three bowlfuls of pastries must confess the offense.
A nun who eats between noon and dawn must confess the offense.
A nun who eats food stored overnight must confess the offense.
A nun who consumes unoffered food or drink must confess the offense.
A nun who takes another nun on almsround, but then tells her to leave because she wants to misbehave must confess the offense.
A nun who sits down intruding on a lustful couple must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 125. Sitting with a man in a concealed place
children.blurb: A nun who sits in a secluded place with a man must confess the offense.
A nun who sits in private with a man must confess the offense.
A nun who visits other lay people before or after an invitational meal must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 128. Accepting an invitation of more than four months
children.blurb: A nun who goes beyond the terms of an invitation to ask for requisites must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 129. Going to see a mobilized army
children.blurb: A nun who goes to see an army in action must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 130. Staying more than three days with an army
children.blurb: A nun who stays with an army for more than three nights must confess the offense.
If, while staying with an army, a nun goes to see the army in action, she must confess the offense.
A nun who consumes alcohol must confess the offense.
A nun who tickles another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who plays in water must confess the offense.
A nun who is disrespectful when rightly corrected about a breach of the monastic code must confess the offense.
A nun who frightens another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who lights a fire to warm herself must confess the offense.
A nun in the central Ganges plain who bathes more than once a fortnight, except when dirty, must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 139. Not making stains on a new robe
children.blurb: A nun who wears a new robe without first marking it must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 140. Using assigned property that has not been relinquished
children.blurb: A nun who uses robe-cloth that has been transferred to a fellow monastic must confess the offense.
A nun who hides the requisites of another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who intentionally kills a living being must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 143. Using water with living beings in it
children.blurb: A nun who uses water that contains living beings must confess the offense.
A nun who tries to reopen an issue of monastic law that has been properly settled must confess the offense.
A nun who travels by arrangement with thieves must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 146. Not giving up a bad view
children.blurb: A nun who says that things declared as obstructive by the Buddha are not actually obstructive must confess the offense.
A nun who lives with a nun who has been ejected for holding the view that things declared as obstructive by the Buddha are not actually obstructive must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 148. Supporting a novice nun with a bad view
children.blurb: A nun who lives with a novice nun who has been expelled for holding the view that things declared as obstructive by the Buddha are not actually obstructive must confess the offense.
A nun who uses a ploy as an excuse for not keeping the monastic code must confess the offense.
A nun who criticizes the monastic code must confess the offense.
A nun who deceptively claims that she did not know a rule in the monastic code must confess the offense.
A nun who in anger strikes another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who in anger physically threatens another nun must confess the offense.
A nun who falsely accuses another nun of an offense entailing suspension must confess the offense.
A nun who intentionally makes another nun anxious must confess the offense.
A nun who eavesdrops on other nuns who are disputing about the Teaching or the monastic law must confess the offense.
A nun who has given her consent for the Order to perform an official act, but then criticizes the act must confess the offense.
A nun who departs without giving her consent while the Order is deciding on an issue must confess the offense.
If a nun, acting as part of a complete Order, gives away a robe to an official of the Order, but then criticizes the act, she must confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 160. Diverting gains intended for the Sangha
children.blurb: A nun who diverts to an individual an offering intended for the Order must confess the offense.
A nun who picks up anything considered valuable must confess the offense.
A nun who makes a needle-case of bone, ivory, or horn must destroy it and confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 163. Making an inappropriate bed or bench
children.blurb: A nun who makes a bed with long legs must shorten them and confess the offense.

children.translated_name: 164. Making an upholstered bed or bench
children.blurb: A nun who makes a bed with luxurious upholstery must remove the upholstery and confess the offense.
A nun who makes a bandage that is too large must cut it down and confess the offense.
A nun who makes a robe that is too large must cut it down and confess the offense.
